key_insight: Parallel Shapley uses Shapley-value reward attribution to give each reasoning path a fair learning signal, reducing noise from redundant or harmful paths in parallel LLM reasoning.
engineering_relevance: For reinforcement learning systems that train multi-path LLMs, this method offers a more stable and interpretable way to allocate credit than uniform outcome-level rewards.
method: Model each reasoning path as a player in a cooperative game.
Estimate path-level utility with a generative reward model.
Approximate Shapley contributions efficiently using Monte Carlo sampling.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved performance over existing baselines on mathematical reasoning benchmarks, with more stable training and better interpretability.
watch_item: Shapley estimation can add computational overhead and depends on the quality of the reward model, which may affect attribution accuracy.
paper_title: Fishing Out Free Riders: Shapley-Based Reward Attribution for Parallel Reasoning via Reinforcement Learning
